source_jobs.title: Software Engineer for Fraud Prevention (TypeScript)
source_jobs.description:
Apify is the largest marketplace of tools for AI. 25,000 Actors helping people and agents get real-time web data, track competitors, generate leads, or integrate their apps. Actors are built by a global creator community that now earns more than $1M every month.

Join us to help people put the web to work. Apify can find missing children https://blog.apify.com/fighting-child-traffickers-with-technology/, protect consumers from fake discounts across the EU https://blog.apify.com/how-web-scraping-ai-and-the-eu-have-come-together-to-sweep-away-fake-discounts-in-europe/, and feed data to AI chatbots https://blog.apify.com/intercom-customer-support-ai-chatbot-web-scraping/.

We're looking for an experienced Engineer to help us detect, prevent, and respond to fraud across the Apify platform. The challenge isn't just catching bad actors. It's doing it without slowing down the legitimate users who depend on us every day.




WHAT YOU'LL BE WORKING ON:

 - Design and build systems for fraud prevention and remediation, balancing customer experience, losses, and implementation costs.

 - Build and refine risk models, rules, and signals that stop abuse in real time.

 - Investigate new attack patterns and deploy mitigations quickly.

 - Develop internal tools and processes to support incident response and investigations.

 - Work closely with Product, Security, and Finance to find the right balance between safety and user experience.

 - Document and share playbooks for common attack types so the whole team can learn from each incident.




WHO WE'RE LOOKING FOR:

 - Hands-on experience in fraud prevention or trust & safety, ideally at scale. You've seen real attacks and know how to respond.

 - Strong engineering background with a focus on reliability and problem-solving.

 - Ability to analyze complex signals and turn them into actionable detection logic.

 - Comfortable doing data analysis and collaborating with the Apify data team.

 - Proactive and practical. You don't wait for someone to tell you what to fix.

 - A clear communicator who can explain trade-offs and document decisions.


NICE TO HAVE:

 - Familiarity with payment systems, chargebacks, or abuse prevention in SaaS/marketplaces.

 - Experience with identity, account integrity, or bot detection.


OUR TECH STACK:

 - Infra: AWS Compute (Kubernetes (EKS), EC2, Lambda), Helm, Terraform, CloudFormation, ArgoCD, MongoDB, Redis, DynamoDB, S3, GitHub Actions

 - Monitoring: Grafana, Prometheus, OpenTelemetry, Mezmo, Sentry, PagerDuty

 - Frontend: React.js, styled-components, Storybook, Chromatic, Cypress, Playwright

 - Backend: TypeScript/Node.js, Nest.js, Next.js, Docusaurus, Vitest

 - Tools: GitHub, Notion, Google Workspace, Slack

 - OS, code editor, and AI assistant of your choice (Claude premium with unlimited tokens, GH Copilot, Cursor, Gemini, or JetBrains AI)

 - Process: two-week sprints, code reviews, tests, automating whatever we can, and deploying multiple times per day.




EXPECTATIONS:




BY THE END OF THE FIRST 3 MONTHS, WE EXPECT YOU TO:

 - Understand the Apify platform end-to-end and ship your own Actor.

 - Ship your first detection rule or risk signal that's actively preventing fraud.

 - Build solid working relationships with the product team you'll be working with.

 - Identify the 3 highest-impact gaps in our fraud prevention and propose solutions.


BY THE END OF THE FIRST 6 MONTHS, WE EXPECT YOU TO:

 - Investigate fraud incidents independently.

 - Own at least two fraud vectors end-to-end (e.g. payments and account abuse).

 - Ship 3-5 detection improvements that measurably reduce fraud without hurting legitimate users.

 - Be a trusted partner to the Product team and the Data engineering/analytics team.

 - Present a clear roadmap for the next 6-12 months of fraud prevention work.




WHY SHOULD YOU WORK AT APIFY?

 - Space, support, and autonomy for personal growth, with a direct impact on our success

 - Full-time position in Prague (Lucerna P

source_jobs.title: Head of Brand
source_jobs.description:
Apify is the largest marketplace of tools for AI. 25,000 Actors helping people and agents get real-time web data, track competitors, generate leads, or integrate their apps. Actors are built by a global creator community that now earns more than $1M every month.

Join us to help people put the web to work. Apify can find missing children https://blog.apify.com/fighting-child-traffickers-with-technology/, protect consumers from fake discounts across the EU https://blog.apify.com/how-web-scraping-ai-and-the-eu-have-come-together-to-sweep-away-fake-discounts-in-europe/, and feed data to AI chatbots https://blog.apify.com/intercom-customer-support-ai-chatbot-web-scraping/.

We’re looking for a Head of Brand to define and amplify Apify's voice in the global market and make Apify a distinctive brand. We need someone who can craft a compelling brand narrative that resonates with developers, enterprise leaders, and the broader AI ecosystem. You will be the architect of how the world perceives Apify. You will drive our presence at global events, shape our social media voice, and launch campaigns that make Apify synonymous with the future of web data.




WHAT YOU'LL DO:

 - Define and orchestrate the long-term brand vision to transform Apify into a global “distinctive brand” that people are proud to use. You will bridge the gap between our roots in web scraping and our future in AI to create a narrative that is both logical and emotionally compelling.

 - Make Actors the industry-standard synonym for web automation tools, AI agents, and web scrapers. Your goal is to make the term "Actor" as ubiquitous in our space as "container" is in DevOps.

 - Build and lead the brand team, shifting our approach from ad-hoc activities to a cohesive, high-impact strategy that amplifies our reach.

 - Move beyond standard marketing to conceptualize and drive the creation of new categories for Apify, executing bold campaigns that drive awareness and sentiment, such as viral community challenges, major partnership announcements, out-of-home advertisements, and digital PR pieces.

 - Act as a strategic partner to the senior team, defining the thought leadership narrative and identifying high-leverage speaking opportunities (podcasts, panels, press) that establish Apify as the category leader.

 - Work hand-in-hand with the marketing leadership to ensure that our brand awareness campaigns and performance acquisition efforts are perfectly synchronized. You will align on messaging and timing so that "Brand" creates the demand that "Performance" captures while “Content” makes sure the messaging is consistent across all touch points.

 - Collaborate closely with the PeopleOps team to shape our employer branding, ensuring Apify is recognized as a top-tier destination for world-class engineering and GTM talent.

 - Use data to prove the value of the brand. You understand that brand isn't just "fluff" and you will build the framework to measure success through share of voice, sentiment analysis, direct traffic lift, and organic search volume.


WHO WE’RE LOOKING FOR:

 - You have 5+ years of experience in brand marketing, ideally at a technology company with a strong focus on software developers and B2B SaaS/PaaS. Experience with the US market is a must, as you understand the cultural nuances required to win there.

 - You have a proven track record of building and managing high-performing teams; you know how to hire, mentor, and lead a team of event managers, social media pros, and creatives.

 - You understand the mechanics of building a distinctive brand. You know that strong brands are built on emotion, not just logic, and you have experience turning standard users into passionate fans and advocates.

 - You have successfully repositioned a company or defined a new market space in the past. You know how to take a product known for "X" and shift the narrative so the market sees it as "Y" (e.g. from a niche tool to a critical platform). You don't just compete

source_jobs.title: SEO specialist (Technical SEO focus)
source_jobs.description:
Apify is the largest marketplace of tools for AI. 25,000 Actors helping people and agents get real-time web data, track competitors, generate leads, or integrate their apps. Actors are built by a global creator community that now earns more than $1M every month.

Join us to help people put the web to work. Apify can find missing children https://blog.apify.com/fighting-child-traffickers-with-technology/, protect consumers from fake discounts across the EU https://blog.apify.com/how-web-scraping-ai-and-the-eu-have-come-together-to-sweep-away-fake-discounts-in-europe/, and feed data to AI chatbots https://blog.apify.com/intercom-customer-support-ai-chatbot-web-scraping/.



We're looking for an SEO Specialist with a technical focus to own the foundation that makes Apify discoverable across search engines and LLMs. Apify's website is a complex ecosystem - product pages, developer docs, a marketplace with thousands of tools, blog posts, programmatic landing pages - and all of it needs to work for users, search engines, and now AI models pulling context from the web.

You'll scale our SEO by building and optimizing programmatic landing pages, setting up systems that automatically catch issues before they become problems, and eliminating manual work through smart workflows. This isn't maintenance mode - it's about building infrastructure that lets our content perform at scale.




WHAT YOU'LL BE WORKING ON:

 - You'll be part of the SEO team, reporting directly to the SEO Manager, and working closely with Content, Engineering, and the Marketing team.

 - Monitor and fix technical SEO issues. You'll own technical SEO health using tools like Ahrefs, Screaming Frog, and Google Search Console.

 - Improve crawlability, indexability, and site architecture. You'll continuously optimize how search engines discover and understand the Apify website.

 - Scale SEO through programmatic and other pages. You'll work on programmatic SEO initiatives, helping create and optimize landing pages at scale using internal tools and tools like AirOps.

 - Implement and maintain structured data. You'll own Schema.org http://Schema.org implementation across different page types (product pages, documentation, FAQs, how-to content).

 - Analyze logs and crawl data to inform strategy. You'll dig into server logs and crawl data to understand how Googlebot and LLM bots interact with the Apify website.

 - Build and maintain SEO automations and workflows. You'll set up SEO automations that save the team hours of manual work and keep us proactive rather than reactive.

 - Beyond technical work, you care about the full picture and will also contribute to overall SEO performance. Researching new topics, analyzing traffic patterns, spotting opportunities for improvement, and contributing to the broader SEO strategy.




WHO WE'RE LOOKING FOR:

 - 3+ years of hands-on SEO experience at a SaaS or tech company, with deep technical SEO expertise: crawlability, indexation, site architecture, canonicalization, hreflang, structured data implementation, robots.txt, XML sitemaps, and log file analysis.

 - You think strategically and see the big picture, coming up with innovative ideas that move the needle at scale. Self-motivated problem solver - if you hit an obstacle, you find a way around it.

 - Experience with Core Web Vitals audits, performance optimization, Schema.org http://Schema.org markup, and JSON-LD implementation. You know how to structure data for rich results and can read server logs to extract actionable insights.

 - Programmatic SEO experience - you've created and optimized thousands of pages at scale while maintaining quality.

 - Proficient with Screaming Frog, Ahrefs, Google Search Console, Google Analytics, and Google Sheets, plus you can set up basic automations in these tools or in Make/n8n.

 - Excellent written and verbal English to explain technical concepts to non-technical teammates. You use AI extensively to work faster and stay on top of SEO's

source_jobs.title: Fullstack Engineer (React/Node.js)
source_jobs.description:
Apify is the largest marketplace of tools for AI. 25,000 Actors helping people and agents get real-time web data, track competitors, generate leads, or integrate their apps. Actors are built by a global creator community that now earns more than $1M every month.

Join us to help people put the web to work. Apify can find missing children https://blog.apify.com/fighting-child-traffickers-with-technology/, protect consumers from fake discounts across the EU https://blog.apify.com/how-web-scraping-ai-and-the-eu-have-come-together-to-sweep-away-fake-discounts-in-europe/, and feed data to AI chatbots https://blog.apify.com/intercom-customer-support-ai-chatbot-web-scraping/.



WHAT YOU’LL BE WORKING ON?

 - We need your help building our product to make it easier for beginners and more powerful for advanced users.

 - You will work in a small cross-functional team carrying features all the way from the discovery phase up until production.

 - And as the company and product keep growing, there will always be plenty of challenges, interesting projects, and unexpected features to create.

 - Apify is built for developers by developers. We’re using the Apify platform ourselves every day, and put great pride in making it better for ourselves too.

 - We are big advocates of open source and publish everything that we can on GitHub https://github.com/apify (PRs are welcome 😉).

Our tech stack:

 - Infra: AWS Compute (Kubernetes (EKS), EC2, Lambda), Helm, Terraform, CloudFormation, ArgoCD, MongoDB, Redis, DynamoDB, S3, GitHub Actions

 - Monitoring: Grafana, Prometheus, OpenTelemetry, Mezmo, Sentry, PagerDuty

 - Frontend: React.js, styled-components, Storybook, Chromatic, Cypress, Playwright

 - Backend: TypeScript/Node.js, Nest.js, Next.js, Docusaurus, Vitest

 - Tools: GitHub, Notion, Google Workspace, Slack

 - OS, code editor, and AI assistant of your choice (Claude premium with unlimited tokens, GH Copilot, Cursor, Gemini, or JetBrains AI)

 - Process: two-week sprints, code reviews, tests, automating whatever we can, and deploying multiple times per day.
   
   


WHO WE’RE LOOKING FOR:

 - A strong technical background and a great professional track record

 - 4+ years of industry experience building the backend and frontend systems

 - Excellent skills at developing and debugging in TypeScript/Node.js or proficient in some other programming language + you're ready to learn JavaScript quickly

 - Knowledge of any technologies mentioned below is a plus

 - A collaborative and experiment-driven mindset

 - Good communication skills in English
   
   


WHY SHOULD YOU WORK AT APIFY?

 - Space, support, and autonomy for personal growth, with a direct impact on our success

 - Full-time position in Prague (Lucerna Palace) or Brno (Titanium) 🏰

 - Option to work remotely 🛋️

 - Flexible working hours (perfect for both night owls 🦉 and early birds 🐥)

 - Nobody counts holidays as long as the work gets done 💪

 - Unlimited Claude for every Apifier. We don't count tokens. Just use them well 🤖

 - Stock options and profit sharing 💰

 - We welcome pets, kids, and bikes at the office 🐕👨‍👧🚲

 - Epic team buildings and offsites 🚢 with biking, canoeing, and other adventures 🪂

 - Solid education and training budget, conference tickets, internal “Eat & Learn” sessions, and the possibility to work across teams 👩🏼‍💻👨🏽‍💻

 - Generous hardware budget 💻

 - Free lunches every day when you’re in the office 🌮🍱🍜🍕🥡

 - Unlimited supply of ☕ & 🍺 and snacks

 - Free entry to the wonderful Prague Zoo 🐘

 - Free Multisport card 🏋

 - Ping-pong, chess, PS5, lightsabers, foosball league after lunch.

For more details about Apify and what it is like to work with us, see our Careers page https://apify.com/jobs.

source_jobs.title: Senior Backend Engineer for proxy and unblocking
source_jobs.description:
Apify is the largest marketplace of tools for AI. 25,000 Actors helping people and agents get real-time web data, track competitors, generate leads, or integrate their apps. Actors are built by a global creator community that now earns more than $1M every month.

Join us to help people put the web to work. Apify can find missing children https://blog.apify.com/fighting-child-traffickers-with-technology/, protect consumers from fake discounts across the EU https://blog.apify.com/how-web-scraping-ai-and-the-eu-have-come-together-to-sweep-away-fake-discounts-in-europe/, and feed data to AI chatbots https://blog.apify.com/intercom-customer-support-ai-chatbot-web-scraping/.

To support this mission, we're looking for a Senior Backend Engineer to work on proxy infrastructure and unblocking.

Anti-bot systems are constantly evolving, and we need to keep our scraping infrastructure ahead of them. You'll expand Apify Proxy, research anti-scraping services, and build internal tools that use browser fingerprinting, human-like interactions, and session management to keep data flowing.

If you enjoy solving hard technical problems and contributing to open-source proxy libraries, this role might be for you.




WHAT YOU'LL BE WORKING ON:

 - Building robots that behave and act like real users to automate human activities on the web

 - Working in a small cross-functional team with other experienced hackers

 - Expanding the capabilities of Apify Proxy, adding more protocols and more underlying providers, and implementing internal unblocking capabilities

 - Researching existing anti-scraping services and implementing new internal services to overcome them by utilizing various browser fingerprinting methods, human-like browser interaction, session management, and others

 - Contributing to proxy-chain https://github.com/apify/proxy-chain and impit https://github.com/apify/impit, our open-source libraries for proxy handling and request impersonation


OUR TECH STACK:

 - Infra: AWS Compute (Kubernetes (EKS), EC2, Lambda), Helm, Terraform, CloudFormation, ArgoCD, MongoDB, Redis, DynamoDB, S3, GitHub Actions

 - Monitoring: Grafana, Prometheus, OpenTelemetry, Mezmo, Sentry, PagerDuty

 - Frontend: React.js, styled-components, Storybook, Chromatic, Cypress, Playwright

 - Backend: TypeScript/Node.js, Nest.js, Next.js, Docusaurus, Vitest

 - Tools: GitHub, Notion, Google Workspace, Slack

 - OS, code editor, and AI assistant of your choice (Claude premium with unlimited tokens, GH Copilot, Cursor, Gemini, or JetBrains AI)

 - Process: two-week sprints, code reviews, tests, automating whatever we can, and deploying multiple times per day.




WHO WE’RE LOOKING FOR:

 - A strong technical background or great professional track record (we're looking for exceptional candidates, no matter the seniority)

 - 2+ years of industry experience building proxy, fingerprinting, or unblocking services

 - Strong skills in developing and debugging with TypeScript/Node.js, or equivalent skills in another language and the ability to learn quickly

 - Familiarity with Linux

 - Experience with AWS, other public clouds, or any technology mentioned above (nice to have)

 - An experiment-driven and collaborative mindset

 - Good communication skills in English




WHY SHOULD YOU WORK AT APIFY?

 - Space, support, and autonomy for personal growth, with a direct impact on Apify's success

 - Full-time position in Prague (Lucerna Palace) or Brno (Titanium) 🏰

 - Option to work remotely 🛋️

 - Flexible working hours (perfect for both night owls 🦉 and early birds 🐥)

 - Nobody counts holidays as long as the work gets done 💪

 - Unlimited Claude for every Apifier. We don't count tokens. Just use them well 🤖

 - Stock options and profit sharing 💰

 - We welcome pets, kids, and bikes at the office 🐕👨‍👧

 - Epic team buildings and offsites 🚢 with biking, canoeing, and other adventures 🪂

 - Solid education and training budget, conference tickets, internal “Eat & Learn” sessio

source_jobs.title: Product Marketing Manager (Apify Platform)
source_jobs.description:
Apify is the largest marketplace of tools for AI. 25,000 Actors helping people and agents get real-time web data, track competitors, generate leads, or integrate their apps. Actors are built by a global creator community that now earns more than $1M every month.

Join us to help people put the web to work. Apify can find missing children https://blog.apify.com/fighting-child-traffickers-with-technology/, protect consumers from fake discounts across the EU https://blog.apify.com/how-web-scraping-ai-and-the-eu-have-come-together-to-sweep-away-fake-discounts-in-europe/, and feed data to AI chatbots https://blog.apify.com/intercom-customer-support-ai-chatbot-web-scraping/.

The Apify platform is the engine that powers everything we do. Now we need someone to own how the world understands it - a Product Marketing Manager to lead go-to-market for Apify's core infrastructure.




WHAT YOU'LL BE WORKING ON:

 - Shape the product roadmap - work closely with the Foundations product group, bringing market and user feedback that influences priorities and feature launches.

 - Own GTM for the Apify platform - develop and execute marketing strategy across compute (batch and server-style Actor runs), Storage (Datasets, key-value stores, request queues), Apify Proxy, the Apify API, Console, and new platform features.

 - Make infrastructure accessible - translate technical concepts like compute units, Actor runtimes, batch vs. server-style Actors, IAM, Webhooks, Schedules, and Proxy chains into clear, compelling narratives for both technical and non-technical audiences.

 - Drive platform and feature adoption - develop messaging frameworks, landing pages, documentation improvements, blog content, and targeted campaigns. Define what "successful adoption" looks like for each platform feature, create in-product and out-of-product touchpoints (changelogs, in-app nudges, email sequences, developer newsletters), and track feature-level engagement metrics to measure uptake over time.

 - Grow Apify's reach through partnerships - work with cross-functional partners on co-marketing initiatives that expand our presence in the developer ecosystem.

 - Stay ahead of the market - track web scraping companies, AI tool marketplaces, and developer tooling trends to sharpen Apify's competitive positioning.

 - Measure and report on campaign performance - using data to inform future strategies and product decisions.

 - Keep everyone aligned - make sure Sales, Marketing, Content, and DevRel are always up to speed on platform capabilities, use cases, and upcoming launches.




WHO WE'RE LOOKING FOR:

 - You've done this before - 5+ years in B2B product marketing, ideally at a developer tools, infrastructure, or cloud platform company, with direct experience working with product managers and engineers.

 - You're technically curious - you're comfortable with developer-facing products like APIs, serverless compute, cloud infrastructure, and SDKs. You don't need to be an engineer, but you need to speak their language.

 - You know how to go to market - you have a proven track record of developing and executing GTM strategies for technical products, including positioning, messaging, campaign delivery, and developer adoption programs.

 - You can make complexity land - you distill complex infrastructure concepts into narratives that resonate, whether you're writing for a solo developer, a data engineering team, or an enterprise architect.

 - You write and communicate with clarity - in English, across formats, for technical and non-technical audiences.

 - Cloud and developer tooling experience is a plus - hands-on familiarity with AWS, GCP, Azure, serverless computing, or developer tooling ecosystems will help you hit the ground running.




WHAT WE EXPECT FROM YOU IN THE FIRST 6 MONTHS:

You'll join as an individual contributor with the Core Services/Platform team. Your first goal is to understand the Apify platform, its products, its users, how they use it, and w

source_jobs.title: Creator Growth Lead
source_jobs.description:
Apify is the largest marketplace of tools for AI. 25,000 Actors helping people and agents get real-time web data, track competitors, generate leads, or integrate their apps. Actors are built by a global creator community that now earns more than $1M every month.

Join us to help people put the web to work. Apify can find missing children https://blog.apify.com/fighting-child-traffickers-with-technology/, protect consumers from fake discounts across the EU https://blog.apify.com/how-web-scraping-ai-and-the-eu-have-come-together-to-sweep-away-fake-discounts-in-europe/, and feed data to AI chatbots https://blog.apify.com/intercom-customer-support-ai-chatbot-web-scraping/.

We're growing fast, and how the world understands what Apify is matters more than ever. That’s why we’re looking for a Creator Growth Lead to own the content creator marketing program from the ground up, building a network of creators and influencers who reach the right audiences, communicate Apify's value accurately, and generate demand across the full funnel.

You'll sit between demand creation and product marketing, treating creators as both a production engine and a distribution layer for the rest of the marketing system. You'll be the single owner of creator-driven results: who we work with, what they make, how it performs, and how it ties back to the funnel.




WHAT YOU'LL BE WORKING ON:



Build and scale the content creator program

 - Source, vet, and activate content creators and influencers across YouTube, LinkedIn, TikTok, X, Reddit, podcasts, and other channels focused on developers, AI builders, and technical decision-makers.

 - Own the program structure: tiers, compensation models (fixed fee, CPA, hybrid), onboarding, and regional execution aligned to GTM priorities.

 - Manage budgets, contracts, rates, and usage rights across all creator partnerships

 - Own channel planning and forecasting.

 - Stay ahead of creator-economy trends, platform shifts, emerging formats, and new talent, and bring that intelligence into how the program evolves.

Connect creators to the broader marketing system

 - Work with Product Marketing to ensure creator content reflects Apify's product narrative and GTM priorities - content creators are an extension of how Apify communicates externally, not a separate voice.

 - Partner with the Affiliate Manager so every creator relationship is structured, tiered, briefed, and tracked, and every partner works from the same content standards and creative direction.

 - Work with the Social Media team to align creator content with the organic content calendar and amplify top-performing content across owned channels.

 - Own program reporting: content velocity, ICP reach, messaging quality, creator-attributed signups, and revenue influence.

 - Own paid placements and UGC buying, routing top-performing content into other channels for amplification.

Own content quality and distribution

 - Brief creators on what to make and why, translating Apify's product positioning and campaign priorities into clear content direction.

 - Track content velocity per creator: posting frequency, video performance, and whether they're active or dormant.

 - Own quality control: messaging accuracy, feature representation, and ICP fit.

 - Build playbooks, creative frameworks, and repeatable systems so quality scales with volume.




WHO WE’RE LOOKING FOR:

 - You have 6+ years in media buying, influencer marketing, or partnerships, ideally within a B2B, developer, gaming, digital product, or technical audience - with proven experience building content creator or influencer programs.

 - You have a sharp eye for what resonates with technical audiences - you know the difference between content that builds genuine credibility and content that reads as paid promotion.

 - You've worked with audiences who are skeptical of marketing by default, and you've built programs that respect that skepticism rather than trying to override it.

 - You understand

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Marketing Manager (for Apify Store)
source_jobs.description:
Apify is the largest marketplace of tools for AI. 25,000 Actors helping people and agents get real-time web data, track competitors, generate leads, or integrate their apps. Actors are built by a global creator community that now earns more than $1M every month.

Join us to help people put the web to work. Apify can find missing children https://blog.apify.com/fighting-child-traffickers-with-technology/, protect consumers from fake discounts across the EU https://blog.apify.com/how-web-scraping-ai-and-the-eu-have-come-together-to-sweep-away-fake-discounts-in-europe/, and feed data to AI chatbots https://blog.apify.com/intercom-customer-support-ai-chatbot-web-scraping/.

We're looking for a Senior Product Marketing Manager to own the go-to-market strategy for Apify Store. You'll focus on the user side: how users find Apify Store, pick the right Actor, run it, and come back. You’ve run and improved an e-commerce shop, marketplace, or app store, and you want to apply that thinking to a marketplace of AI and web scraping tools.




WHAT YOU'LL BE WORKING ON:

 - Owning the communication strategy for Apify Store - messaging, positioning, and campaigns. Working with Product on how users find Apify Store, choose an Actor, and become active users.

 - Working with the Discovery team to improve how users find the right Actor - through search, categories, featured placements, and recommendations. Bringing user and market feedback directly into product decisions.

 - Planning and running marketing campaigns for the Apify Store homepage and launches - covering Store features, high-profile Actors, and promotion of the Apify platform. Working with the other PMMs so Apify Store reads as a coherent product rather than a list of separate launches.

 - Owning the marketing of Store collections - groups of Actors organized around a specific user problem or use case, so users go from "I need something for X" to running an Actor in minutes.

 - Understanding what users come to Apify Store to do, what gets in their way, and why they pick one Actor over another. Turning that into messaging, positioning, and marketing content per user persona and use case.

 - Defining, measuring, and reporting on the metrics for Apify Store marketing: user sign-ups, activation, trial-to-paid conversion, retention, and Store-level growth.

 - Watching competing platforms and app stores. Turning what you learn into concrete recommendations for Apify Store, and keeping internal teams in the loop on how Apify Store is performing and where it sits in the market.




WHO WE'RE LOOKING FOR:

 - 5+ years in product marketing or a related user-side role, ideally where you’ve owned a chunk of the funnel.

 - You've grown an e-commerce shop, marketplace, or app store, and you know how to improve the numbers that matter: discovery, conversion, retention, and repeat purchase.

 - You can take a marketplace apart and see what makes individual products sell, then turn that into campaigns that help users find and start using the right Actors.

 - You turn data into clear next actions. You know which metrics matter for marketplace growth, how to set up the right tracking, and you've built reporting frameworks before.

 - You pick the small number of things that will move Apify Store's growth, and ship them.

 - You can run several campaigns at once, and you work well with Product, Engineering, Marketing, and Developer Relations.

 - You write and speak English clearly and confidently.

 - Familiarity with web scraping, automation, AI agents, or API products is a strong plus.




BY THE END OF THE FIRST THREE MONTHS, WE EXPECT YOU TO:

You'll start as a senior individual contributor (IC) working with the Apify Store and Discovery teams. Your first job is to understand how users reach Apify Store, what they're trying to get done, and what stands between them and running an Actor successfully.

You’ll be talking to people across the company constantly, so we’d like you to spend most of

source_jobs.title: Data Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Apify is the largest marketplace of tools for AI. 30,000+ Actors helping people and agents get real-time web data, track competitors, generate leads, or integrate their apps. Actors are built by a global creator community that now earns more than $1M every month.

Join us to help people put the web to work. Apify can find missing children https://blog.apify.com/fighting-child-traffickers-with-technology/, protect consumers from fake discounts across the EU https://blog.apify.com/how-web-scraping-ai-and-the-eu-have-come-together-to-sweep-away-fake-discounts-in-europe/, and feed data to AI chatbots https://blog.apify.com/intercom-customer-support-ai-chatbot-web-scraping/.

We're looking for a Data Engineer to own the integration layer between Snowflake and the operational tools that run Apify's go-to-market and product motion: HubSpot, Intercom, Mixpanel, and Segment. You'll make sure the right data lands in the right system at the right time, with the right shape, so Sales, Marketing, Customer Success, and Product teams can act on it.

You'll be the 9th member of the data team - joining a mix of analytical engineers, analysts, and data scientists - at the moment Segment is being rolled out as Apify's CDP. That's yours to land end-to-end.



WHAT YOU'LL BE WORKING ON:

 - Own the integration domain end to end - all pipelines, transformations, and Snowflake models that connect HubSpot, Intercom, Mixpanel, and Segment to the rest of the platform, in both directions.

 - Design event tracking and the CDP layer with the RevOps team as Segment becomes the source of truth for behavioral data flowing into product, marketing, and CRM systems.

 - Build reliable, observable pipelines in Keboola and dbt - with clear data contracts, schema tests, freshness guarantees, and alerting.

 - Model integration data in Snowflake so HubSpot, Intercom, Mixpanel, and Segment data lands in well-defined tables that downstream consumers can trust, with documentation that analysts and scientists can actually use.

 - Power lifecycle automations - PQA scores back into HubSpot, behavioral campaigns in Intercom and customer.io http://customer.io, product usage signals - by shipping the data they depend on.

 - Diagnose and resolve pipeline incidents independently - trace lineage across multiple components, find root causes, fix, and write the runbook so it doesn't bite the next person.


TECH STACK

 - Snowflake - data warehouse

 - Keboola - extractors, writers, and orchestration

 - dbt - transformations on Snowflake (orchestrated by Keboola; this is where we're actively migrating existing transformation logic)

 - Tableau and Redash - BI

 - n8n - workflow automation

 - Segment - CDP, currently being rolled out end-to-end




WHO WE'RE LOOKING FOR:

 - 3+ years of data engineering experience, with meaningful time spent on integrations between a cloud warehouse and operational SaaS tools (HubSpot, Salesforce, Intercom, Zendesk, Mixpanel, Amplitude, Segment, RudderStack, or similar).

 - Fluent in SQL (window functions, CTEs, complex multi-source joins, query optimization) and comfortable in Python for the parts a no-code tool can't handle.

 - Production experience with Snowflake (or BigQuery, Databricks, Redshift), and an understanding of the cost, performance, and access-control tradeoffs of a usage-based warehouse.

 - Experience building end-to-end pipelines combining an orchestration or ELT platform (Keboola, Fivetran, Airflow, Dagster, Prefect, Matillion) with a transformation framework like dbt.

 - Hands-on experience with a CDP (Segment, RudderStack, mParticle) - tracking plans, schemas, identity resolution, downstream consumers - not just installing the snippet.

 - You think in data contracts - schema stability, freshness SLAs, documented field definitions - and treat the boundary between your domain and downstream consumers as a first-class interface.

 - Comfortable with reverse ETL (Census, Keboola, or hand-rolled), and you understand what

source_jobs.title: Product Marketing Manager (Integrations)
source_jobs.description:
Apify is a marketplace for AI tools, the place where developers, AI builders, and data teams find, run, and build tools that power their agents and AI workflows. We started as a web scraping platform. Today, we're the infrastructure behind data extraction pipelines, MCP integrations, AI agents, and automated workflows that teams around the world depend on.

Apify collects billions of rows of data from all over the world for science https://apify.com/success-stories/helping-the-planet-with-data, market research https://apify.com/success-stories/automated-competitive-intelligence, or finding lost children https://apify.com/success-stories/fighting-child-traffickers-with-technology. We make it possible for AI chatbots https://blog.apify.com/intercom-customer-support-ai-chatbot-web-scraping/ to interact intelligently, and we're actively involved in developing novel ways to work with generative AI like ChatGPT https://blog.apify.com/add-custom-actions-to-your-gpts/.

To support this mission, we're looking for a Product Marketing Manager to help us build and promote Apify's integrations with platforms like n8n, Make, Zapier, and others.




WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING:

 - Work hand in hand with the Integrations team to provide market and user feedback that influences the future roadmap of Apify's integrations. Collaborate daily with the Product Manager on open projects, activities, and roadmap priorities.

 - Own marketing/GTM strategy for Apify's integrations, from high-level positioning to tactical execution. You'll work on initial launches and co-marketing campaigns that drive adoption and awareness.

 - Expand partnerships with key platforms such as Make, Zapier, and n8n - coordinating joint campaigns, tutorials, and content initiatives

 - Understand the needs of Apify’s user base - from individual developers to large enterprises - to create product messaging, positioning, and marketing collateral for our integrations.

 - Measure and report on the performance of marketing campaigns, using data to inform future strategies and product decisions.

 - Collaborate with the broader PMM team to share learnings, align messaging frameworks, and contribute to customer journey optimization across clusters.

 - Identify key integration trends and monitor the competitive landscape to find new opportunities for growth and partnerships.

 - Keep internal teams (Product, Sales, and Marketing) informed and educated about new integrations and use cases.




WHO WE’RE LOOKING FOR:

 - You have 5+ years of experience in B2B product marketing, ideally at a technology company with a strong focus on developers and a product-led growth (PLG) model.

 - You have a technical background and/or experience in the integration/automation ecosystem (Zapier, Make, or n8n, and others) and experience leading co-marketing with external partners.

 - You have a proven track record of developing and executing GTM and product marketing strategies, including user research, competitive analysis, and messaging and campaign delivery.

 - You can take complex technical concepts (APIs, webhooks, MCP) and turn them into marketing stories that land with any audience.

 - You are comfortable being in front of the camera for co-marketing webinars, tutorials, or speaking at events.

 - Integrations, automations, and APIs genuinely excite you. You're always looking for new ways to use them.

 - You work well across teams and know how to build trust and keep communication clear with Product, Marketing, and Partner teams.

 - You have excellent written and verbal communication skills in English.

 - You set your own direction and keep it aligned with the company and integrations team.




WHAT WE EXPECT FROM YOU IN THE FIRST 6 MONTHS:

You'll start as an individual contributor in the Integrations team. Your first goal is to become familiar with the team's workflow (sprint planning, retrospectives, and other meetings), then agree on a strategy before launching major marketing initiativ

source_jobs.title: Integration Partnership Manager
source_jobs.description:
Apify is the largest marketplace of tools for AI. Thousands of Actors helping people and agents get real-time web data, track competitors, generate leads, or integrate their apps. Actors are built by a global creator community that now earns more than $1.2M every month. Join us to help people put the web to work. Apify can find missing children https://blog.apify.com/fighting-child-traffickers-with-technology/, protect consumers from fake discounts across the EU https://blog.apify.com/how-web-scraping-ai-and-the-eu-have-come-together-to-sweep-away-fake-discounts-in-europe/, and feed data to AI chatbots https://blog.apify.com/intercom-customer-support-ai-chatbot-web-scraping/.



Apify has become an integral part of many automation and AI agent platforms. As that ecosystem grows, so does the opportunity to deepen our presence across the integration and workflow automation platforms our users rely on every day. We're looking for an Integrations Partner Manager to grow and manage this expanding segment of our partner network. You'll report to the partnerships lead, work alongside the existing partnerships team, and own a distinct portfolio of platform partners.




WHAT YOU'LL BE WORKING ON:

 - Own and grow a portfolio of platform partners, serving as their central point of contact across every engagement.

 - Run the full partnership lifecycle, from outreach and qualification through to signed agreements, across both integration and strategic co-sell deals.

 - Develop joint value propositions, go-to-market plans, and co-selling motions with each partner.

 - Track the partner pipeline and partner-influenced revenue in HubSpot, keeping a clear line of sight on what each partnership contributes.

 - Coordinate across sales, marketing, product, and engineering, and feed partner needs into the product and integration roadmap.

 - Build and scale the systems behind partnerships: playbooks, onboarding, and partner enablement.
   
   


WHO WE'RE LOOKING FOR:

Must-haves:

 - 2+ years in partnerships, business development, or alliances at a B2B SaaS company.

 - A track record of building partnerships that drove measurable revenue, not just signed logos.

 - Comfortable discussing API-based integrations with both technical and business audiences, and enough technical insight to know what makes an integration genuinely valuable.

 - Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English.

 - The ability to run many partnerships at once and prioritize them by strategic value rather than treating them all the same.

 - You’re ready to own early-stage partnerships where the playbook isn't written yet, and the judgment to build structure as you go.
   
   

Nice-to-haves:

 - Familiarity with the integration and automation ecosystem (Zapier, Make, n8n, or similar) and how listings, connectors, and templates work.

 - Hands-on experience with HubSpot, or a comparable CRM, for pipeline and attribution tracking.

 - Exposure to the AI agent space (MCP, LLM tooling, or agentic frameworks) and why real-time web data matters to it.

Don't worry if you don't meet all of the above criteria. We value diverse skills and experience and would love to hear from you.




BY THE END OF THE FIRST 3 MONTHS, WE EXPECT YOU TO:

 - Build a working knowledge of Apify's product, competitors, current partners, and the partner types we work with.

 - Audit Apify's presence across the major integration and automation platforms, mapping existing listings, connectors, templates, and the gaps worth closing.

 - Define an ideal partner profile and deliver/update a prioritized target list.

 - Own partner pipeline tracking in HubSpot, associating deals with partners so partner-influenced revenue can be reported.

 - Kick off at least one pilot or expanded collaboration with a selected partner.

 - Present a portfolio roadmap with clear short-term and long-term targets.
   
   


BY THE END OF THE FIRST 6 MONTHS, WE EXPECT YOU TO:

 - Establish or deepen 2-3 plat

source_jobs.title: Senior Backend Engineer for Creators group (Node.js/TypeScript)
source_jobs.description:
Apify is the largest marketplace of tools for AI. On Apify Store https://apify.com/store, tens of thousands of Actors https://apify.com/actors, purpose-built tools for the web, help people and AI agents get real-time data, track competitors, generate leads, and connect apps. Anyone can build one and earn from it.

Apify is looking for a Senior Backend Engineer (Node.js/TypeScript). As a member of the Creators group, you'll be building a world-class ecosystem for community developers and builders to create, publish, and monetize their ideas as Actors on Apify. You will build tools and features that help them succeed in the ongoing AI revolution, utilize agentic coding and other agentic flows in their daily operations, and earn monthly payouts to support their living (collectively earning >$1.2M/month from usage of their Actors!).




WHAT YOU'LL BE WORKING ON:

 - Own features and projects end-to-end, from the discovery phase through to production, and see them have a real impact on creators.

 - Build the tools and features creators rely on to publish, monetize, and grow their Actors.

 - Work in a small, cross-functional team, shipping in two-week sprints and deploying daily.

 - Use the Apify platform yourself every day - we build for builders by builders - and take pride in making it better.

 - Contribute to open source; we publish everything we can on GitHub https://github.com/apify (PRs are welcome 😉).

You will be joining one of the following teams:




PUBLISHERS TEAM

The Publishers team builds everything a creator needs once they publish their Actor on Apify and make it available in Apify Store. They own our Actor monetization logic, analytics, review system, systems for maintaining Actor quality, and much more. By joining the Publishers team, you'll work on the next generation of tools and features that help creators around the world use AI to run a business and earn a living on Apify.

📍 Location: flexible, onsite in Prague for ~2 days/week at minimum




BILLING TEAM

The Billing team is responsible for the entire payout infrastructure for creators, ensuring that >$1.2M/month actually reaches the hands of hundreds of community developers. They also own the entire billing system, ensuring Apify users can subscribe, invoices are issued, payments go through, and more. One of the most exciting challenges you'll work on is building the backbone for the emerging agentic payment economy, alongside deep, complex work to keep our internal billing system running efficiently and reliably as Apify's business grows.

📍 Location: onsite in Prague or Brno / fully remote; if remote, visiting our office at least once a month and onboarding (~1-2 weeks) in Prague




WHO WE'RE LOOKING FOR:

 - 3+ years of industry experience building backend systems and APIs.

 - Strong development and debugging skills in TypeScript/Node.js, or proficiency in another language, and the drive to learn TypeScript quickly.

 - Hands-on experience with AI tools in your day-to-day work, and the drive to stay up to date with the latest trends.

 - An experiment-driven, collaborative mindset.

 - A strong technical background or a great professional track record.

 - Good communication skills in English.


OUR TECH STACK:

 - Infra: AWS Compute (Kubernetes (EKS), EC2, Lambda), Helm, Terraform, CloudFormation, ArgoCD, MongoDB, Redis, DynamoDB, S3, GitHub Actions

 - Monitoring: Grafana, Prometheus, OpenTelemetry, Mezmo, Sentry, PagerDuty

 - Frontend: React.js, styled-components, Storybook, Chromatic, Cypress, Playwright

 - Backend: TypeScript/Node.js, Nest.js, Next.js, Docusaurus, Vitest

 - Tools: GitHub, Notion, Google Workspace, Slack

 - OS, code editor, and AI assistant of your choice (Claude premium with unlimited tokens, GH Copilot, Cursor, Gemini, or JetBrains AI)

 - Process: two-week sprints, code reviews, tests, automating whatever we can, and deploying multiple times per day.


WHY SHOULD YOU WORK AT APIFY?

 - Space, support, and autonomy for

source_jobs.title: Support Engineer (afternoon/night shifts)
source_jobs.description:
Apify is the largest marketplace of tools for AI. 25,000 Actors helping people and agents get real-time web data, track competitors, generate leads, or integrate their apps. Actors are built by a global creator community that now earns more than $1M every month.

Join us to help people put the web to work. Apify can find missing children https://blog.apify.com/fighting-child-traffickers-with-technology/, protect consumers from fake discounts across the EU https://blog.apify.com/how-web-scraping-ai-and-the-eu-have-come-together-to-sweep-away-fake-discounts-in-europe/, and feed data to AI chatbots https://blog.apify.com/intercom-customer-support-ai-chatbot-web-scraping/.

To support this mission, Apify is looking for a Night Shift Support Engineer to handle customer inquiries from 5 PM to 2 AM CET. You'll solve both business and technical issues via chat and email, while mastering our web scraping platform. If you have programming knowledge, strong communication skills, and prefer evening hours, join our team in Prague, Brno, or remotely in Czech republic.




WHAT YOU’LL BE WORKING ON:

Our Support team processes customer questions and other internal notifications every day. Besides Apify Console, our main tools are Intercom for external tickets and Slack for internal notifications and discussions.

The conversations cover a wide range of both business and technical topics, for example:

 - What we consider business:
   
   - Choosing the right subscription plan
   
   - Help with invoices and billing
   
   - Help to understand Apify features
   
   - Processing user compensation

 - What we consider technical:
   
   - Help with integrations
   
   - Coding help with Apify API/SDK
   
   - Help with Apify-maintained Actors

When our AI bot processes the external conversations, it divides them into different priorities based on various criteria. Our team then holds different SLAs based on the priority levels. Our main goals concern customer experience. It’s namely the customer satisfaction and response timeliness. Apify’s customers can be both businesses and end-users, but we’re generally used to a B2C setup, with a larger volume of shorter engagements.

From internal notifications, we have a set of topics that are challenging to automate because they need human insight, but need to be processed in a timely manner. They typically are related to processing certain billing issues or preventing fraudsters from exploiting the platform.

We always provide only written support, either via chat or email. Our Support team doesn't provide phone support or on-site visits.

Besides providing support, we also get our feet wet with other projects. For example, we’re really into automation and simplification of processes, AI in Support, building knowledge in Help Center, and also expanding our technical insights.

As Apify grows, we’d like to expand to cover American timezones. At the same time, we’d like to keep our multicultural and multilingual but Czech-based culture. That’s why we’re looking for a night owl. The office time will typically be from 5 PM to 2 AM CET on workdays. You can do that from one of our offices in Prague, Brno, or fully remotely. We may occasionally want you to come sooner or to participate in one of our company events.

As we are expanding our support coverage, we may hold shifts for public holidays and also weekends for the most urgent conversations.




WHO WE'RE LOOKING FOR:

 - 1–2 years of support experience, ideally in a software-as-a-service company

 - Knowledge of programming principles and HTML/CSS/JavaScript is an advantage, interest in technology at the very least

 - Preferences to work in the afternoons/evenings (from 5 PM to 2 AM CET) with holiday/weekend standby duties

 - Passion for technology

 - Ability to explain complex information to customers clearly and concisely

 - Professional verbal and written communication skills in English

 - Strong customer orientation




BY THE END OF THE FIRST

source_jobs.title: Platform Reliability Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Apify is the largest marketplace of tools for AI. 40,000+ Actors helping people and agents get real-time web data, track competitors, generate leads, or integrate their apps. Actors are built by a global creator community that now earns more than $1.2 million every month.



Join us to help people put the web to work. Apify can find missing children https://blog.apify.com/fighting-child-traffickers-with-technology/, protect consumers from fake discounts across the EU https://blog.apify.com/how-web-scraping-ai-and-the-eu-have-come-together-to-sweep-away-fake-discounts-in-europe/, and feed data to AI chatbots https://blog.apify.com/intercom-customer-support-ai-chatbot-web-scraping/.



To support our mission, we're looking for a Platform Reliability Engineer with a developer's mindset. You've shipped code and you care what happens when it runs in production (speed, failures, recovery). You'll help us strengthen how Apify monitors systems, handle incidents, and route alerts so engineering teams can ship with confidence. You won't be on-call. This role is focused on sustainable improvement, not after-hours emergency response.




WHAT YOU'LL BE WORKING ON:

 - Monitoring & signals: Operate and improve our monitoring stack (Prometheus, Grafana, OpenTelemetry) - instrument services to expose the right metrics, define what we watch in production, and shape alerting so teams get actionable signals without the noise.

 - When things go wrong: Help define how we run incidents - clear communication, structured learning afterward, and supporting artifacts (status page, runbooks).

 - With the team: Work with platform and product engineers to make reliability standards practical - help teams adopt better tooling or practices when things change, and write documentation people actually use.
   
   


WHO WE'RE LOOKING FOR:

 - You have hands-on experience choosing what to measure in production - not just reading dashboards, but picking signals that reflect the customer experience.

 - You're comfortable with incidents and alerts, from early detection through resolution and follow-up so similar issues are less likely to recur.

 - You have hands-on experience with Prometheus, Grafana, OpenTelemetry, or similar, and with alert-routing tools such as PagerDuty.

 - You read and write code: you can follow services and pipelines across the stack and collaborate on technical details with the teams building them.

 - You know what good post-incident culture looks like in practice - blame-free, learning-focused, and actually used to make things better - even if your past title never mentioned reliability.

 - You can write clear, concise guidance that teams adopt, and you work constructively toward sound decisions.

 - You're driven to automate repetitive tasks and improve developer workflows.
   
   

Nice to have:

 - Meaningful hands-on experience as an application or backend developer - you've built things that run in production and approach observability as someone who needs it as a "user," not just the person who sets it up.

 - Experience building and maintaining infrastructure on AWS (EC2, EKS, S3, CloudFormation, or similar), and hands-on experience with container technologies.

 - Some familiarity with CI/CD pipelines or release practices - enough to have an informed opinion on what makes deployments reliable and safe.

Don't worry if you don't meet all of the above criteria. We value diverse skills and experience and would love to hear from you.



Our tech stack

 - Infra: AWS Compute (Kubernetes (EKS), EC2, Lambda), Helm, ArgoCD, MongoDB, Redis, DynamoDB, S3, GitHub Actions

 - Monitoring: Grafana, Prometheus, OpenTelemetry, Mezmo, PagerDuty

 - Frontend: React.js, styled-components, Storybook, Chromatic, Cypress, Playwright

 - Backend: TypeScript/Node.js, Nest.js, Next.js, Express.js, Docusaurus, Vitest

 - Tools: GitHub, Notion, Google Workspace

 - Editor and AI assistant of your choice (GH Copilot, Cursor, Claude, Gemini, or JetBrai

Apify can find missing children https://blog.apify.com/fighting-child-traffickers-with-technology/, protect consumers from fake discounts across the EU https://blog.apify.com/how-web-scraping-ai-and-the-eu-have-come-together-to-sweep-away-fake-discounts-in-europe/, and feed data to AI chatbots https://blog.apify.com/intercom-customer-support-ai-chatbot-web-scraping/.

To support this mission, we're looking for a Senior Backend Engineer to join the Universe team. You'll build and scale the Actors that power AI use cases across Apify - including tools like Website Content Crawler https://apify.com/apify/website-content-crawler, which turns any website into clean data for AI models, vector databases, and RAG pipelines. If you care deeply about code quality, enjoy reverse-engineering how the web works, and want to shape the technical direction of a small, ambitious team - this is for you.




WHAT YOU'LL BE WORKING ON:

 - Building and scaling Website Content Crawler https://apify.com/apify/website-content-crawler and other core Actors that crawl any website reliably under real-world load. That means deep-crawling pages, stripping out the noise, and turning them into clean text, Markdown, and structured data that feed AI models, vector databases, and RAG pipelines - with a stack of AI tools doing the heavy lifting under the hood

 - Working in a small, cross-functional team alongside other experienced hackers

 - Contributing to Crawlee, Apify's open-source web scraping library, and stepping into product engineering when a project needs platform-level work. It won't be your day-to-day, but you'll jump in on these cross-team projects whenever they need a hand rather than handing the problem off

 - Writing clean, elegant code that reads like a novel, and keeping it that way through reviews and refactoring

 - Reverse-engineering how websites work behind the scenes, and designing APIs other developers love to use

 - Using AI as a real part of the job, and building it too. AI is changing how web automation gets built, so you'll both put it to work day to day (writing code faster, prototyping, solving extraction problems that were impractical before) and help build the AI-powered tools that make the rest of the team and the wider community faster. You'll get a Claude Code license to do it

 - Growing into ownership over time - leading initiatives, mentoring, and shaping the technical direction of the team: setting architecture, deciding how separate projects and tools connect into a coherent whole, and keeping the bigger picture sound as the codebase grows


WHO WE'RE LOOKING FOR:

Must-haves:

 - You're strong in JavaScript and TypeScript (Node.js) and understand web technologies well

 - You have around 2 to 4 years of professional or open-source experience on a non-trivial project built with a team

 - You treat code quality, readability, and frequent refactoring as part of getting the job done, not an afterthought

 - You already use AI tools in your work and understand why they matter. You know where they help, where they don't, and you treat getting good at them as part of staying a strong engineer

 - You have a problem-solving mindset and an appetite to keep learning, in web scraping and beyond

 - You think in systems. You can design architecture, weigh trade-offs, and see how separate projects fit together into a coherent technical product

 - You communicate clearly in English, in writing and in speech

Nice to have:

 - Experience leading a team or project, formally or informally, whether by managing people or just guiding and unblocking the ones around you

 - A track record of contributing to a non-trivial,

source_jobs.title: AI Adoption Lead
source_jobs.description:
Apify is the largest marketplace of tools for AI. 40,000+ Actors helping people and agents get real-time web data, track competitors, generate leads, or integrate their apps. Actors are built by a global creator community that now earns more than $1.2 million every month.

Join us to help people put the web to work. Apify can find missing children https://blog.apify.com/fighting-child-traffickers-with-technology/, protect consumers from fake discounts across the EU https://blog.apify.com/how-web-scraping-ai-and-the-eu-have-come-together-to-sweep-away-fake-discounts-in-europe/, and feed data to AI chatbots https://blog.apify.com/intercom-customer-support-ai-chatbot-web-scraping/.

AI is core to how Apify works and competes. The majority of companies run on Claude (Chat, Code, Cowork), but we are keeping a diverse offering of other tools, such as Gemini, ChatGPT, Cursor, n8n, and many others. Our budget is projected in the hundreds of thousands of dollars a year. We've moved fast, and AI is embedded in many functions. We run weekly office hours, have strong adopters on every team, and a shared Claude context repo. But this is only the foundation, and there's much more to build. Some people still don't know where to start or what's expected of them. We've come a long way, and see a huge opportunity to go much further. That's why this role exists.

We're looking for an AI Adoption Lead to drive how AI gets used across Apify. This is a role about people, tools, and practices, not about owning a single tool.

You'll work directly with team leaders across Marketing, Legal, People, and beyond to define what "AI proficient" means for each role, and turn what works in one team into something every team can use. Reporting to the CTO, you'll own the rituals, shared context, and security guardrails that make fast, confident AI adoption possible.




WHAT YOU'LL BE WORKING ON:

 - Meeting individual teams regularly to learn their AI blockers and wins, then carry proven practices to the rest of the company.

 - Partner with team leaders to define role-based AI proficiency expectations and help them achieve them.

 - Run the recurring rituals that inspire others and provide help (office hours, demos, hackathons), and grow a company-wide use case database.

 - Merge Apify's scattered AI context into a single company-wide skills marketplace, and lower the barrier for teams to contribute their own skills.

 - Own a secure, cost-aware default setup for Claude and the rest of the AI stack, working with Security and Legal on policy and guardrails.

 - Track AI spend, usage per team, and build the first clear view of the ROI AI adoption delivers.

 - Help people separate real AI value from hype and mitigate FOMO, keeping adoption grounded in what actually works.


WHO WE'RE LOOKING FOR:

Must-haves:

 - You are a very experienced daily user of agentic tools like Claude or similar and able to show, not just tell, how to use them well.

 - Comfortable talking to both technical and non-technical teams (Engineering, Marketing, Legal, Finance) and adjusting your approach for each.

 - Experience driving the adoption of tools, improving processes, or practices across multiple teams in a company, not just within your own team.

 - Strong facilitation skills, able to run a workshop, demo, or hackathon, and get people to actually use what they learn.

 - Comfortable working without direct authority, influencing team leaders and adopters rather than managing them.

 - Working understanding of AI security and cost considerations (data governance, access control, spend tracking).

Nice-to-haves:

 - Experience building or maintaining an internal knowledge base or skills marketplace.

 - Background in developer relations, enablement, or technical program management.

 - Experience partnering with security or legal teams on tooling policy.


WHY SHOULD YOU WORK AT APIFY?

 - Space, support, and autonomy for personal growth, with a direct impact on Apify's success

source_jobs.title: Senior Partner Marketing Manager
source_jobs.description:
Apify is the largest marketplace of tools for AI, the place where developers, AI builders, and data teams find, run, and build tools that power their agents and AI workflows. We started as a web scraping platform. Today, we're the infrastructure behind data extraction pipelines, MCP integrations, AI agents, and automated workflows that teams around the world depend on.

We're growing fast, and our partner ecosystem is a big part of how we scale. We work with industry leaders, including AWS, Snowflake, Databricks, Clay, and others, and these relationships are becoming a core part of how Apify reaches new audiences, drives pipeline, and wins in the market. That's why we're looking for a Sr. Partner Marketing Manager to own these relationships from a marketing perspective and make sure we're getting the most out of every one of them.




WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING:

 - Build and execute joint GTM plans with key technology partners (AWS, Snowflake, Databricks, Clay, and others), from messaging and co-branded assets to sponsored placements, events, and pipeline-generating campaigns.

 - Develop joint partner value propositions and make sure they're consistent everywhere partners show up, including co-sell motions with the right positioning and enablement content.

 - Drive Apify's presence across partner platforms, like AWS or Snowflake marketplace, keeping content, descriptions, assets, and positioning always current and on-brand.

 - Own the partner marketing budget and manage marketing development funds (MDF) so Apify gets the most value out of every opportunity: track what's coming in, allocate funds strategically.

 - Work closely with Product and Sales to make sure partner marketing reflects the product roadmap and that Sales always has what they need to close partner-sourced deals.

 - Act as the primary marketing contact for Apify Partners and partner teams, running regular check-ins and making sure things actually move.

 - Maintain a shared source of truth for partner marketing: active initiatives, guidelines, best practices, and asset libraries.

 - Represent Apify at co-hosted partner events, hackathons, or focused meetups, and coordinate the narrative and presence with the partners and broader internal GTM team.




WHO WE'RE LOOKING FOR:

 - You have at least 4 years in partner marketing, channel marketing, or B2B marketing in a SaaS or developer tooling environment.

 - You can translate a high-level partnership into a concrete marketing plan and then actually execute it. You're as comfortable building a campaign brief as you are chasing a logo update on AWS Marketplace.

 - You're organized and proactive. Keeping track of things comes naturally to you.

 - You have strong written communication skills and can create partner-facing materials that are clear, on-brand, and genuinely useful.

 - You're comfortable working across a lot of stakeholders at once - internal teams, partner managers, product and marketing teams - and you know how to align them without losing momentum.

 - You understand performance metrics and can report on the impact of partner marketing activities in terms that matter to the business (pipeline, revenue influence, marketplace traffic).

 - Bonus: you have experience with developer-focused audiences, AI/ML tooling ecosystems, or cloud marketplaces.




WHAT WE EXPECT FROM YOU IN THE FIRST 6 MONTHS:

Your first goal is to understand the Apify platform, its products, its users, how they use it, and why they love it. The role sits across multiple teams. You'll work closely with the Partnership and Core Services Product teams.

You'll start by mapping the current state of partner marketing at Apify: what exists, what's missing, and where the biggest opportunities are. There's no polished playbook to inherit. Your first job is to audit, prioritize, and start building.




BY THE END OF THE FIRST MONTH, WE EXPECT YOU TO:

 - Complete onboarding, meet key stakeholders, and audit what's already happening acr

notes: High tech role ratio, frequent postings, and a strong focus on engineering and product development suggest an engineering-heavy organization.
